steps.title: Read two endpoint values from the line of best fit
steps.content: The line of best fit spans $x=0$ to $x=10$ hours. Reading from the graph, two clear points on the line are approximately $(0,175)$ and $(10,575)$.

steps.title: Compute the slope
steps.content: Using the two readable points, the slope is $$\frac{575-175}{10-0}=\frac{400}{10}=40.$$ The line predicts an increase of about $40^\circ\text{F}$ per hour.

steps.title: Match the slope and direction to the choices
steps.content: The slope is positive, so the predicted temperature increases, not decreases. Its size is about $40^\circ\text{F}$ per hour, not $170^\circ\text{F}$ per hour. Choice C — The predicted temperature increases at a constant rate of approximately 40°F per hour over the 10-hour period. states both the correct direction and the correct rate.
